displayTitle: The Light Is a Claim, the Meter Is the Proof
kicker: RAD SAFETY // NEUTRON OPERATING & EMERGENCY PROCEDURES

beats.narration: Neutron radiography happens at a fixed facility, not out of a truck. The hazards are engineered into the building, so the safety lives in written procedures.

payload.title: NEUTRON WORK IS PROCEDURE WORK
payload.sub: Fixed facility. Mixed field. Written rules.

beats.narration: Three things make it different from gamma work. The field is mixed, neutrons plus gamma. Ordinary meters miss neutrons. And the beam leaves radioactivity behind.

payload.heading: Why neutron work is its own animal

tiles.s: Neutron rem meter, not a gamma badge

beats.narration: Close every run the same way. The status light claims the shutter shut. A neutron meter at the door is the only proof.

payload.heading: Close the run, then prove it at the door
payload.note: NEUTRONS AT THE DOOR = SHUTTER FAILED — DO NOT ENTER

beats.narration: Then survey what the beam touched, and know the emergencies cold: a stuck shutter, a power loss, someone inside the cell, contamination, fire.

payload.heading: After the run — and when it goes wrong

chips.s: Kill the beam, get them out

beats.narration: So one rule underwrites all of it. Indicator lights make claims. Surveys give proof. And your facility's own written procedures are always the final word.

payload.sub: Both meters, every entry — your facility's procedures are the final word.
